ddls:
CREATE TABLE "devices" (
  "device_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each device */,
  "device_name" TEXT /* Name of the device */,
  "device_type" TEXT /* Type of the device */,
  "device_description" TEXT /* Description of the device */,
  "manufacturer" TEXT /* Manufacturer of the device */,
  "model_number" TEXT /* Model number of the device */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "device_registers" (
  "device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the device */,
  "register_id" INTEGER /* ID of the register */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("device_id", "register_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "registers" (
  "register_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each register */,
  "address" TEXT /* Hexadecimal address of the register */,
  "register_name" TEXT /* Name of the register */,
  "bit_size" INTEGER /* Size of the register in bits */,
  "type" TEXT /* Type of the register (e.g., read-only, read-write) */,
  "function" TEXT /* Function of the register */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the register */,
  "data_type" TEXT /* Data type of the register (e.g., binary, hexadecimal, decimal) */,
  "min_value" INTEGER /* Minimum value of the register */,
  "max_value" INTEGER /* Maximum value of the register */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("register_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "register_versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each register version */,
  "register_id" INTEGER /* ID of the register that the version belongs to */,
  "version" TEXT /* Version number of the register */,
  "update_date" TEXT /* Date when the register was updated */,
  "update_description" TEXT /* Description of the update */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_register_versions_register_id FOREIGN KEY ("register_id") REFERENCES registers ("register_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "register_values" (
  "value_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each register value */,
  "register_id" INTEGER /* ID of the register that the value belongs to */,
  "value" TEXT /* Value of the register */,
  "data_type" TEXT /* Data type of the value (e.g., binary, hexadecimal, decimal) */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the value was recorded */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the value */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("value_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_register_values_register_id FOREIGN KEY ("register_id") REFERENCES registers ("register_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access log */,
  "register_id" INTEGER /* ID of the register that was accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who accessed the register */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the register was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., read, write) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the access */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_register_id FOREIGN KEY ("register_id") REFERENCES registers ("register_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., engineer, developer, admin) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the user's last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "role_description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id", "role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "permissions" (
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each permission */,
  "permission_name" TEXT /* Name of the permission */,
  "permission_description" TEXT /* Description of the permission */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("permission_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "role_permissions" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role */,
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* ID of the permission */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id", "permission_id")
)
